cards.back: A mathematical model consisting of a state space, action space, transition model, and reward function, used to define a problem for reinforcement learning
The set of all possible states that the agent can be in, used to describe the current situation
The set of all possible actions that the agent can take, used to describe the possible choices
A function that describes the probability of transitioning from one state to another given an action
A function that assigns a numerical reward to the agent for taking a particular action in a given state, used to guide the agent's decisions
